title: ImageInfo carries ICC/EXIF/XMP Uint8Arrays that are structured-cloned on every decode_progress and decode_final message
description: ImageInfo embeds iccProfile (Uint8Array), exif (Uint8Array), and xmp (Uint8Array). These fields are included verbatim in MsgDecodeProgress.info and MsgDecodeFinal.info (protocol.ts lines 94, 113). Because they are Uint8Array (not ArrayBuffer), they cannot be listed in the transferables array and are therefore structured-cloned by the MessageChannel on every frame. For a progressive decode with N passes this means N full ICC copies (up to ~4 KB each) crossing the worker boundary even though the profile never changes after the header. The correct approach is to send ImageInfo without the blob fields in progress/final frames and rely on the single MsgDecodeHeader (or a one-time side-channel) for ICC/EXIF/XMP, or convert them to ArrayBuffer and transfer once.

title: MsgDecodeProgress repeats full ImageInfo (including binary blobs) on every progressive frame
description: MsgDecodeProgress carries info: ImageInfo on every progress event (line 94). The static fields (width, height, bitsPerSample, hasAlpha, hasAnimation, jpegReconstructionAvailable) never change after decode_header, and the blob fields (iccProfile, exif, xmp) are especially expensive to clone repeatedly. For a 12 MP image with 4 progressive passes and a 3 KB ICC profile, this is ~12 KB of unnecessary structured-clone work per decode. A lighter ProgressInfo type omitting the blobs (or omitting info entirely on progress frames and letting the session cache it from decode_header) would eliminate this overhead.

title: No IPC payload size budget / regression test for protocol message sizes
description: The protocol defines large union types (MainToWorkerMessage, WorkerToMainMessage) but there is no automated check that prevents accidental addition of large inline payloads (like the current ImageInfo blobs) to high-frequency messages. A small CI step that measures serialized size of synthetic MsgDecodeProgress/MsgDecodeFinal objects and asserts a ceiling (e.g. < 1 KB excluding the pixel ArrayBuffer) would catch regressions early and document the intended payload budget.
code_excerpt:
export type WorkerToMainMessage =
  | MsgDecodeHeader
  | MsgDecodeProgress
  | MsgDecodeFinal
  | MsgDecodeError
  | MsgDecodeCancelled
  | MsgDecodePaused
  | MsgDecodeBudgetExceeded
  | MsgEncodeChunk
  | MsgEncodeFirstByteReady
  | MsgEncodeDone
  | MsgEncodeError
  | MsgEncodeCancelled
  | MsgWorkerReady
  | MsgWorkerShutdownAck
  | MsgWorkerError
  | MsgWorkerDrain
  | MsgMetric;
